findings.title: Bucket versioning disabled
findings.description: Storage bucket does not have object versioning enabled.
findings.risk: Without versioning, accidental deletion or overwrite of objects is permanent. An attacker with write access could silently replace data.

remediation.description: Enable object versioning on the bucket.

findings.title: User-managed service account key
findings.description: Service account has a user-managed key older than 90 days.
findings.risk: User-managed keys are long-lived credentials that can be exfiltrated. Workload Identity Federation eliminates this risk.

remediation.description: Delete the user-managed key and use Workload Identity Federation.
cli_commands: gcloud iam service-accounts keys delete KEY_ID --iam-account=deploy@prod-123.iam.gserviceaccount.com

findings.title: Bucket allows public access
findings.description: Storage bucket has allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers in its IAM policy.
findings.risk: Any internet user can read the bucket contents, which may contain sensitive data.

remediation.description: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers from the bucket IAM policy.

findings.title: No required reviews on default branch
findings.description: Default branch does not require pull request reviews before merging.
findings.risk: A compromised account could push malicious code directly to production without peer review.

remediation.description: Enable required reviews on the default branch protection rule.

findings.title: Unpinned GitHub Actions
findings.description: Workflow uses actions referenced by mutable tag instead of SHA pin.
findings.risk: A supply chain attack on the action repository could inject malicious code into CI/CD pipelines.

remediation.description: Pin all GitHub Actions to full commit SHAs.

attack_paths.title: Supply chain: unpinned action -> infrastructure repo -> production GCP
